pages.text:
E.A. 39.

23/73

REPUBLIC OF SOUTH AFRICA.

REPUBLIC

MINISTERIE VAN BUITELANDSE SAKE,

MINISTRY OF FOREIGN AFFAIRS,

CAPE TOWN.

31 Jaumany 1966.

Your Excellency,

. 2nd and 3rd September,

1965, between delegations representing the French and

South African Governinents, at which amendments to the Air

Agreement oi 17th September, 1954, in the following terms were agreed to, and signifying that the French Government has since approved these amendments:

(a)

to suspend articles 4,5,6.7,8 and 9 of the agreement between

Governments, In

regard to air services between their respective territories, which was entered into on 14 September 1956 and 24 September 1956, 4 April 1961 and 4 August 1961 and 22 June 1963, for the period during which an agreement is in force between the airline designated by the Government of the Republic of south Alrica and the airline designated by the Government of France which has been approved by both the said Governments and which relate in particular to the routes, the frequencies and the capacities of the air services to be provided by the said airlines in terms of the firstmentioned agreement.

f the said parties and such consultation shal.

ommence within a period of sixty days reckones

I desire to confirm that the foregoing correctly

expresses

the understanding between our two Governments

His Excellency Mr. Georges Balay,

Ambassador of France,

CAPE IOWN.

and that your letter and this reply are regarded as constituting an agreement between the two Governments by which the existing agreement dated the 17th September, 1954, is amended.

of ny highest consideratioo.

Your Excellency, the assurance

MINISTER OF FOREIGN

AFFAIRS.
